channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Playing by the rules of AI
item.description: The Fast Company Impact Council is a private membership community of influential leaders, experts, executives, and entrepreneurs who share their insights with our audience. Members pay annual membership dues for access to peer learning and thought leadership opportunities, events and more.
item.dc:creator: Juan Orlandini, Fast Company

item.title: Musk’s plan to replace government workers with AI could create chaos
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk’s plan to cut $2 trillion in US government spending apparently includes replacing tens of thousands of federal workers with AI, but the move could produce disastrous results, several AI experts say.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft unveils company's first quantum computing chip
item.description: Microsoft on Wednesday revealed its first quantum computing chip, Majorana 1, stating it will speed up the development of quantum computers able to solve "meaningful, industrial-scale problems in years, not decades.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: This AI trend lets TikTok users relive history’s best—and worst—moments
item.description: Ever wondered what it would be like to wake up in Pompei on eruption day? How about how it would have felt to be a passenger on the Titanic? Now you don’t need to.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Rivian’s CEO talks AI, self-driving cars, and a $5.8 billion partnership with Volkswagen
item.description: As demand for EVs declines, electric automaker Rivian is taking this time to adapt its business and expand its brand.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: This AI tool could help curb domestic violence
item.description: A new technology can pinpoint victims of intimate partner violence four years earlier than other detection systems and with 80% accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Sibani Ram, Fast Company

item.title: VMware by Broadcom: Product, service and support news
item.description: News of Broadcom’s plans to acquire VMware triggered concerns among customers, partners, and industry watchers, many of whom recalled Broadcom’s track record with prior high-profile acquisitions, including network switching vendor Brocade in 2016 for almost $6 billion, software firm CA Technologies
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Everything new on Hulu in March 2025 – including Sean Baker's Oscar-nominated drama Anora
item.description: Anora is just one of the new movies coming to Hulu in March 2025. Here's the full list of the titles coming to the platform next month.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Confidence in air safety slips after DC crash: Survey
item.description: Confidence in air safety and federal government agencies that help sustain it has dropped in 2025 after the deadly Reagan National Airport crash near Washington that killed 67 people, according to a new survey that was released on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Data Protection Concerns: South Korea Accuses DeepSeek of Sharing Citizens' Data with ByteDance
item.description: South Korea's Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC) accuses DeepSeek, a Chinese AI chatbot, of sharing citizens' data with ByteDance. The allegations come as Western governments grow increasingly concerned about the processing of their citizens' data by such companies.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Apple TV+ releases a gritty new crime drama trailer for Dope Thief that looks like a stylish version of The Wire
item.description: Apple TV+'s violent Philadelphia-set show has snagged the legendary Hollywood helmer and will start airing in March.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: After leaving OpenAI, Mira Murati debuts her AI startup, Thinking Machines Lab
item.description: Thinking Machines Lab, an AI startup founded by former OpenAI Chief Technology Officer Mira Murati, has tapped about 30 leading researchers and engineers from competitors such as OpenAI, Meta and Mistral, it said in a blog post on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Airbnb sues New Orleans after it adopts sweeping regulations governing short-term rentals
item.description: Airbnb is suing the City of New Orleans for requiring the company and other short-term rental platforms ensure properties they market are in compliance with city regulations.
item.dc:creator: Jack Brook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Need a bodyguard? Now there’s an app for that
item.description: A new app makes hiring security as simple as ordering an Uber. A viral TikTok with 11.2 million views reads, “POV we just ordered bodyguards to pick us up from the airport.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Virginia Tech researchers raise red flags about mixed-reality security
item.description: In a new study, researchers at Virginia Tech spelled out a variety of security hazards that could compromise mixed-reality systems. The researchers highlighted security threats involving the manipulation of virtual objects when users collaborated via mixed reality headsets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The unbearable lateness of Apple Intelligence
item.description: Apple has apparently delayed what is arguably its most important Apple Intelligence feature, contextual intelligence, by at least another month. It’s the latest chapter in what history will remember as the company’s most painfully slow, yet strategically significant, introduction yet.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The EU's New Competition Commissioner: A Shift in Relations with the US
item.description: The European Union's new Competition Commissioner, Teresa Ribera, has announced a shift in relations with the US, emphasizing reliability and stability. The move comes as President Donald Trump has criticized the EU for its rules and regulations.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 3 reasons to consider a data security posture management platform
item.description: A week rarely goes by without a major data security breach. Recent news includes a breach impacting an energy company’s 8 million customers, another compromising the information on 450,000 current and former students, and one more exposing 240,000 credit union members.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Serverless was never a cure-all
item.description: An obituary for serverless computing might read something like this: In loving memory of serverless computing, born from the fervent hopes of developers and cloud architects alike.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Selective Service using TMF as springboard to DevSecOps
item.description: Scott Jones, the CIO of the Selective Service System, said almost $6 million from the Technology Modernization Fund is accelerating a major IT initiative. The post Selective Service using TMF as springboard to DevSecOps first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Dell secures deal worth more than $5B in Elon Musk's xAI startup company
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI is one step closer to becoming a reality after Dell secured a deal worth more than $5 billion.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, NewsNation

item.title: GenAI can make us dumber
item.description: Generative AI (genAI) tools based on deep learning are quickly gaining adoption, but their use is raising concerns about how they affect human thought.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
